summary.intent: Address three P2 findings from Codex's round-5 review of PR #696 (audit-agnostic-navigation-components): fix optimistic state mutation in controlled steps, remove unspecified future-state language from the accordion spec, and add duplicate-key validation to the TreeView ChildrenLoaded event handler.
summary.outcome: All three fixes were implemented, tested, and merged: controlled last-step completion now skips optimistic mutation; accordion spec no longer references a future Animating state; ChildrenLoaded rejects payloads with duplicate keys. 572 navigation tests pass, clippy clean, xci-fast (all 10 steps) passed, committed and pushed.

repo: Controlled-step mode requires notify-only semantics — the parent owns step state. Every navigation path routes through step_change_plan which is notify-only when controlled; the last-step branch must match that contract by checking ctx.step.is_controlled() and skipping optimistic statuses[active] = Complete mutations.
The repo has a spec rule against deferral language — mentions of 'potential future extensions' or unspecified future states are not allowed in spec files.

TreeCollection::new silently accepts duplicate nodes but get() resolves only to the last occurrence, meaning duplicate keys from ChildrenLoaded can leave focus/selection/ARIA ids resolving to the wrong row.
The project uses cargo xtask spec validate and cargo test -p xtask --test spec_corpus_compile_snippets as part of the CI gate to validate spec markdown and compile spec snippets.
The xci-fast alias (cargo xci --profile fast) runs a 10-step CI suite used as the final gate before committing.
PR review follows a multi-round Codex review cycle on GitHub PR #696, with threads tracked by round (R5-T1, R5-T2, R5-T3) and resolved after fixes are pushed.

code.finding: The NextStep last-step branch was unconditionally setting statuses[active] = Complete. Fixed to check ctx.step.is_controlled() and skip the optimistic mutation in controlled mode, only firing Effect::Complete.

Added loaded_keys_are_unique helper near collect_default_expanded that walks the loaded subtree and rejects payloads whose keys collide with the current collection OR duplicate each other; drops the delivery (parent stays Loading) on failure.
Added children_loaded_rejects_payload_with_duplicate_keys test covering both collide-with-tree and dup-within-payload cases.
Removed 'potential future extension (e.g., an Animating state)' from the State design-note to comply with the no-deferral-language spec rule.

workflow: The Codex review loop polls GitHub GraphQL API for unresolved review threads authored by Codex, processes them by round, implements fixes, runs CI gate (xci-fast), commits, pushes, replies on threads, resolves, then re-triggers Codex.
cargo fmt --all is run before the final test/clippy sweep to avoid formatting-only clippy errors.
The commit message uses a structured multi-bullet format referencing each Codex thread ID (e.g., #696-R5-T1) and includes refs to related issues (#223 #696) for traceability.
Test count tracking (572 nav tests) is used as a sanity check to confirm no tests were lost between rounds.

friction: A clippy -D warnings error appeared after the initial implementation pass, requiring an additional edit to tree_view/mod.rs and a re-run before the suite was clean.
The xci-fast log had to be tailed from a specific section (=== CI Summary ===) because the full output was too large to inspect directly.
open_items: Codex re-trigger after round-5 fixes — the session ends just after push, with Codex re-triggering implied but not yet confirmed as having no further threads.
PR #696 review threads for R5-T1, R5-T2, R5-T3 need replies and resolution posted on GitHub (the commit is pushed but the transcript ends before that step is confirmed complete).
